props.text: TL;DR: Hyperliquid HIP-4 went live on mainnet with zero-fee BTC binaries; Nevada granted a preliminary injunction forcing Kalshi to geofence sports/elections/entertainment by May 4; Roundhill's six election ETFs hit brokerage accounts May 5.

props.title: Hyperliquid HIP-4 outcome markets go live on mainnet, BTC binaries trading immediately

props.text:
First contract — "BTC above 78,213 on May 3 at 11:30 AM" — drew ~89,253 shares in the first six hours, already topping the same-name Polymarket market's ~79,500. Zero open-fee, USDH-collateralized, no liquidation; sports, politics, macro and entertainment slated next.

Why it matters: a third major venue with native crypto onboarding hits the prediction-market tape just as Polymarket asks the CFTC for US re-entry — day-one volume parity on the same BTC contract is a sharp test of how thick the incumbent moat actually is.

props.title: Nevada judge grants preliminary injunction; Kalshi must geofence sports, elections, entertainment by May 4

props.text:
Carson City Judge Jason Woodbury sided with the Nevada Gaming Control Board, calling Kalshi's sports event-contracts "indistinguishable" from licensed-sportsbook bets. Kalshi has until Monday to deploy geolocation or formally seek extension.

Why it matters: first US state to land a hard-edge geofencing order against a CFTC designated contract market — the template state regulators in Utah, Minnesota and Massachusetts will copy if their statutes stall in legislature.

props.title: Roundhill's six election prediction-market ETFs effective May 5

props.text:
BLUP/REDP track the 2028 presidential race; BLUS/REDS the 2026 Senate; BLUH/REDH the 2026 House. All gain exposure via swap agreements on Kalshi binary contracts. Bitwise and GraniteShares filed parallel slates expected to launch in the same window.

Why it matters: first prediction-market exposure available inside standard brokerage and retirement accounts — pulls a new buyer cohort into Kalshi's order book and tests whether ETF-wrapped event contracts hold their NAV through low-liquidity windows.

props.title: Polymarket crossed $76B lifetime notional volume on May 2, with recent monthly trading reportedly running $8–10B
